disclaimer: Document attestation, PRO, translation, and Chamber fees change. Verify before quoting. CRITICAL: the UAE is NOT a Hague Apostille member as of April 2026. Foreign documents still require the full 5-step legalization chain.

attestation.apostille_correction_note: The claim that the UAE acceded to the Hague Apostille Convention on 7 June 2024 is FALSE. Verified against the HCCH official status table for Convention #12 and multiple legal firms. MOFAIC continues to require the full legalization chain for foreign documents.

legalization_chain.name: Home country Ministry of Foreign Affairs (or State Department)
UAE Embassy in the home country
UAE MOFAIC in Dubai or Abu Dhabi
Translation into Arabic by an MOJ-licensed sworn translator (if destination is a UAE court, RERA, or government counter)

documents_typically_attested: Foreign criminal record / good conduct certificate

what_a_pro_does: Visa processing (entry permit, status change, stamping)
pro_services.when_you_need_one: Mainland licenses with 3+ visas typically have an in-house PRO. Smaller setups outsource to PRO firms.
pro_services.pro_vs_setup_consultant_vs_csp: PRO handles operational paperwork after incorporation. Setup consultant handles incorporation itself. Corporate services provider does both plus accounting and compliance.

mandatory_for: Documents submitted in Arabic to UAE courts
Documents submitted to MOFA, RERA, or government counters
legal_translation.registry: Ministry of Justice MOJ-licensed sworn translators

chamber_of_commerce.name: Dubai Chamber of Commerce
chamber_of_commerce.mandatory_for: Mainland commercial license holders (DET commercial activity)
chamber_of_commerce.fee_notes: Tier from AED 700 floor to AED 2,200 top (banks and foreign branches at the top).

common_mistakes.title: Choosing the wrong activity code
common_mistakes.impact: Limits banking, invoicing, and hiring downstream. Cannot be changed without a full license amendment.
common_mistakes.fix: Look up DET activity list on app.invest.dubai.ae before applying. If unsure, choose a broader activity that covers your intended business.

common_mistakes.title: Picking the cheapest free zone without checking bank acceptance
common_mistakes.impact: Meydan and IFZA are cheap to set up but have documented bank account difficulties. You may save AED 5,000 on the license and lose 8 weeks on banking.
common_mistakes.fix: Cross-reference the free zone choice with the bank you intend to use. Use Meydan's in-house bank assistance desk if you must use Meydan.

common_mistakes.title: Forgetting that free zone companies cannot freely invoice mainland UAE B2C
common_mistakes.impact: Many founders set up in a free zone for the cheap license, then discover they cannot directly bill UAE consumers without a local distributor or dual license.
common_mistakes.fix: If you need to invoice mainland UAE B2C, choose mainland (DET) or use a free zone with dual-license capability.

common_mistakes.title: Underestimating the AED 5,000 to 15,000 in PRO and translation fees
common_mistakes.impact: These costs are not in the headline license price and often surprise first-time founders.
common_mistakes.fix: Budget AED 5,000 to 15,000 on top of the license cost for PRO services, MOFA attestation, and legal translation.

common_mistakes.title: Not budgeting for the 1 to 16 week bank account delay
common_mistakes.impact: Founders cannot operate, pay employees, or process payments without a bank account.
common_mistakes.fix: Apply to 2 or 3 banks in parallel. Consider Wio for fast onboarding while traditional banks process.

common_mistakes.title: Using a single-shareholder structure when banks prefer 2+
common_mistakes.impact: Banks scrutinise sole-shareholder setups more heavily and reject them more often.
common_mistakes.fix: If feasible, add a co-founder or family member as a 2% to 10% shareholder to soften the bank profile.

common_mistakes.title: Not getting Ejari before applying for visa quota
common_mistakes.impact: Ejari is a precondition for visa quota in mainland setups. Delays the entire visa pipeline.
common_mistakes.fix: Sign and register Ejari first, then start the visa application chain.

common_mistakes.title: Choosing a high-risk industry without preparation
common_mistakes.impact: Crypto, forex, jewelry, used cars, money service businesses face heavy KYC and frequent rejection.
common_mistakes.fix: Prepare extensive source-of-funds documentation, choose a free zone aligned with your industry, and consider VARA licensing for crypto.

common_mistakes.title: Letting the license lapse
common_mistakes.impact: AED 5,000 one-time reactivation fine, AED 250 per month ongoing penalty, plus immigration consequences for sponsored visas.
common_mistakes.fix: Set a calendar reminder 60 days before renewal.

common_mistakes.title: Trying to operate without UAE Pass in 2025/2026
common_mistakes.impact: UAE Pass is required for almost every government e-service. Operating without it forces you to use slow physical channels.
common_mistakes.fix: Register for UAE Pass on day one. Verify to the highest level (full verification with Emirates ID + biometrics).

common_mistakes.title: Mixing up the three Dubai Chambers
common_mistakes.impact: Joining the wrong chamber wastes the membership fee and provides irrelevant services.
common_mistakes.fix: Mainland commercial license holders join Dubai Chamber of Commerce by default. International trade focus joins Dubai International Chamber. Tech/digital startups engage with DCDE through programs (no standalone membership).
